title: Use Punch-In Effects
description: Learn to use the EP-133's Punch-In FX 2.0 for live performance effects and transitions.

steps.title: Start a Pattern Playing
steps.instruction: Make sure you have a pattern with some sounds in it. Press PLAY to start playback.
steps.tip: Use the beat you created in the 'Build a Beat' workflow, or load any project with sounds.

steps.title: Try Beat Repeat
steps.instruction: While the pattern plays, hold pad 1 to engage the Beat Repeat effect. The audio will loop a short section. Release to return to normal.
steps.tip: Press harder for shorter, more intense loops. Lighter pressure gives longer loops.

steps.title: Try Tape Stop
steps.instruction: Hold pad 3 to engage Tape Stop. The audio will slow down dramatically like stopping a tape machine. Release to snap back to normal.
steps.tip: Great for creating tension before a drop!

steps.title: Try Filter Sweep
steps.instruction: Hold pad 5 to engage Filter Sweep. A dramatic filter movement will be applied to the mix. Pressure controls the intensity.
steps.tip: Use this for build-ups or to add movement to a static pattern.

steps.title: Combine Effects with Timing
steps.instruction: Practice triggering Punch-In FX at musical moments: before a new section, on the 4th bar, or during transitions. Timing is everything!

substeps.instruction: Try Beat Repeat (1) to build tension before a drop
Use Tape Stop (3) right before introducing a new element
Apply Filter Sweep (5) over 4 bars for a gradual build

outcomes: Understanding of Punch-In FX 2.0 and pressure sensitivity
Ability to apply live performance effects
Knowledge of when to use effects for musical impact
